The classification of Nuttallia speciosa reflects its unique characteristics and position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Nuttallia speciosa, also known as Chinese Angelica, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, characterized by its tall stems and distinctive umbrella-shaped inflorescence. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Nuttallia speciosa contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including ferulic acid, angelan, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
